growth_records.strain: enrichment from Elanora WWTP waste activated sludge (parent reactor 55C, 3-day SRT)
growth_records.medium: Basic anaerobic (BA) medium + waste-activated-sludge biomass + acetate

up.method: acetate depletion (GC-FFAP) / biomass VS; Monod model fit in Aquasim

sec.units: linear CH4 production rate (g COD/L/d) measured by GC-TCD; ~40% via acetoclastic, ~60% via SAO-HM at 55C (per ref 21)

growth_records.cite: Ho D et al. High-rate, High Temperature Acetotrophic Methanogenesis Governed by a Three Population Consortium in Anaerobic Bioreactors. PLoS ONE (2016). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0159760. [PMC4973872]
growth_records.snippet: Acetate uptake rates were estimated at 0.76 +/- 0.027 g COD g VS-1 d-1 at 55C and decreased to 0.30 +/- 0.035 g COD g VS-1 d-1 at 60C and 65C (average measurement from experimental triplicates).
growth_records.notes: Batch specific-activity test on a thermophilic acetate-degrading prokaryotic consortium (co-culture; parent reactor operated continuously at 3-day SRT so D=1/3 d-1=0.0139 h-1, but this batch test is not a chemostat). Measured acetate uptake 0.76 +/- 0.027 g COD/gVS/d at 55C (experimental triplicate). Unit conversion: acetate 1.067 gCOD/g; 0.76/1.067=0.712 g acetate/gVS/d; /60 g/mol=0.01187 mol=11.87 mmol/gVS/d; /24=0.495 mmol/gVS/h (VS approximates organic gDW). pH not stated for batch bottles. CH4 produced (methanogenesis) but no per-biomass secretion rate tabulated. Rate consistent with prior Ho et al. ref 21.

growth_records.strain: enrichment from Elanora WWTP waste activated sludge (parent reactor 60C, 3-day SRT)
growth_records.medium: Basic anaerobic (BA) medium + waste-activated-sludge biomass + acetate

sec.units: CH4 via SAO-HM dominant at 60C (biogas GC-TCD)

growth_records.cite: Ho D et al. High-rate, High Temperature Acetotrophic Methanogenesis Governed by a Three Population Consortium in Anaerobic Bioreactors. PLoS ONE (2016). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0159760. [PMC4973872]
growth_records.snippet: Acetate uptake rates ... decreased to 0.30 +/- 0.035 g COD g VS-1 d-1 at 60C and 65C. A short lag phase of 1 to 2 days was observed at the higher incubation temperatures.
growth_records.notes: Batch activity test at 60C, consortium co-culture. Measured acetate uptake 0.30 +/- 0.035 g COD/gVS/d. Conversion: 0.30/1.067=0.281 g acetate/gVS/d; /60=0.004686 mol=4.686 mmol/gVS/d; /24=0.195 mmol/gVS/h. Community shifting to Methanothermobacter (hydrogenotrophic) with SAO route becoming dominant. pH not stated.

growth_records.strain: enrichment from Elanora WWTP waste activated sludge (parent reactor 65C, 3-day SRT)
growth_records.medium: Basic anaerobic (BA) medium + waste-activated-sludge biomass + acetate

sec.units: CH4 almost entirely (>90%) via syntrophic acetate oxidation + hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis (per ref 21)

growth_records.cite: Ho D et al. High-rate, High Temperature Acetotrophic Methanogenesis Governed by a Three Population Consortium in Anaerobic Bioreactors. PLoS ONE (2016). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0159760. [PMC4973872]
growth_records.snippet: Acetate uptake rates ... decreased to 0.30 +/- 0.035 g COD g VS-1 d-1 at 60C and 65C ... SAO-HM accounting for ... almost all acetate conversion (>90%) at 65C.
growth_records.notes: Batch activity test at 65C, consortium co-culture. Measured acetate uptake 0.30 +/- 0.035 g COD/gVS/d (same as 60C). Conversion: 0.30/1.067=0.281 g/gVS/d; /60=4.686 mmol/gVS/d; /24=0.195 mmol/gVS/h. >90% of acetate converted via SAO-HM at 65C (cited from Ho et al. ref 21). pH not stated.

carbon_growth.medium: Basic anaerobic (BA) medium + waste-activated-sludge biomass + acetate
carbon_growth.citation: Ho D et al. High-rate, High Temperature Acetotrophic Methanogenesis Governed by a Three Population Consortium in Anaerobic Bioreactors. PLoS ONE (2016). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.01
